evidence_quotes.quote: stop cutting off access to games customers have already payed for.

paragraphs.text: As a EU- Citizen and many others in this feedback section and i would like to suggest that this initiative should include the goals of the Stop Killing Games initiative. Which asks for videogame publishers to stop cutting off access to games customers have already payed for. Video games are so much more than just entertainmentthey're a living, vibrant art form that reflects our society, our technology, and our creativity. When a game is lost, especially a digital one, we lose a small piece of our shared cultural heritage. It's really no different than if a film, a book, or a piece of music were to disappear forever. Its a sad thought that future generations might never get the chance to experience or study these works, leaving a gap in our collective understanding of media history. Many players invest a lot of time, effort, and even money into games, and the idea of them becoming unplayable due to server shutdowns or development cancellations can be really frustrating. It highlights the desire for preservation and continued access to the digital experiences we've come to love.
